title: Will ICE Change Under Its New Leader?
summary: Markwayne Mullin, the new homeland security secretary, has promised a different approach, but how much change is likely? Our reporter Hamed Aleaziz describes what we know.

title: Florida’s Immigration Crackdown, Led by DeSantis, Is Showing Cracks
summary: Some conservative sheriffs have raised concerns about the aggressive enforcement tactics that Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, has embraced.

summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

title: UAE and Qatar Arrest Hundreds Over Online Videos of Iranian Attacks
summary: The authorities have made a wave of arrests to stop people posting footage of strikes, citing security risks. Experts also see a fear of damage to the countries’ image as safe havens.

summary: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump’s improvisational approach.

summary: President Trump said on Thursday he would delay an imminent deadline to begin attacking Iran’s power grid.

summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s highly unusual decision to remove officers from a one-star promotion list has spurred allegations of racial and gender bias.

summary: President Trump is poised to be the first sitting president to have his signature appear on the U.S. dollar.

summary: Since the start of the war in Iran, the average price of gas in the United States has climbed by nearly a dollar.

summary: After a fatal collision between an Air Canada jet and a fire truck on a runway, attention has focused on why the truck didn’t heed frantic calls to stop.

summary: A generation ago, foreign fury over the Iraq invasion often blurred into anti-Americanism. Now, some Europeans seem ready to distinguish between the president and the American people.

summary: Thousands of demonstrations against the Trump administration are scheduled to take place in cities and towns across the country on Saturday.

summary: NBC said that she would return as an anchor of the show on April 6, a little more than two months after the disappearance of her mother.

summary: The war in the Middle East risks worsening an inflation problem that the Federal Reserve has struggled for years to subdue.

summary: With a third of the global supply offline because of the war in Iran, gas companies are scrambling to assure critical A.I. chip makers there will be no disruptions.

summary: It takes a lot of fuel to produce this delicate fruit, which can be a sensitive barometer as oil costs rise.

title: In Maine, Many Older Women Prefer the Younger Man for Senate
summary: Women in their 60s and beyond will likely play a key role in choosing which Democrat will face Senator Susan Collins: Gov. Janet Mills, 78, or Graham Platner, 41.

summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul of New York has demonstrated her ability to negotiate with the president and his team on thorny issues like immigration enforcement.

summary: Voters in Virginia will decide on April 21 whether to redraw the state’s congressional map. Turnout is even higher, thus far, than it was in last year’s governor’s election.

title: Kennedy’s Vaccine Agenda Hits Roadblocks, Diminishing His Clout
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine skepticism is posing challenges for the Trump administration. Top health jobs are unfilled, and a court has blocked his vaccine schedule changes.

summary: Though the health secretary falsely claimed a victory in a recent podcast, a federal proposal to review all new food ingredients is still in the early stages and drawing fierce opposition.

summary: Their anger goes far beyond the recent rise of unemployment and the looming threat of A.I.

summary: We explain the implications of A.I. being so sycophantic.

summary: “How many fake trophies that were made specifically for him is this guy going to get?” Seth Meyers asked on Thursday’s “Late Night.”

title: Nepal’s New Prime Minister, Former Rapper Balendra Shah, Sworn In
summary: A leaked report on the deadly violence and mass arson that broke out last year has put the new leader under pressure to ensure accountability.

title: Humpback Whale Is Freed After Days Stranded Off German Beach
summary: In an unexpected breakthrough, the 40-foot mammal swam to deeper water on Friday morning through a channel that rescuers had dug for it.

title: The Faroe Islands, Wary After Greenland, Vote for Change
summary: Statehood had been a key issue in this tiny Danish archipelago before President Trump threatened Greenland. Now, Faroese voters are focused more on their own economy than geopolitics.

title: U.K. Police to Reinvestigate Sex Crime Allegations Against Andrew Tate
summary: A police force in England said it would look again at allegations against the influencer from 11 years ago. Mr. Tate was not charged in the case and denies any wrongdoing.
